messages.content: Hello, Can you please improve the following sentence?
Sure, please provide the sentence that needs improvement.

You are correct in saying that the face amount of your policy is on decline schedule. The policy is designed to reduce the face amount from the initial amount of $4,986,000 to $10,000 over time. By reducing the face amount, we are able to reduce the related cost of insurance. 

The death benefit, however, is the combination of the face amount and cash value of the policy. The goal of this type of the policy is ultimately to have the low insurance amount (reducing face amount  low cost) and let your own deposits (premium/cash value) make up the most of death benefit. In the beginning of the policy year, the face amount is high with low cash value. Over time, this allocation switches to low face amount with higher cash value. 

The initial plan was to deposit $1,000,000 over the first 5 years ($200,000 x 5 years). At that time we used an investment assumption of 2.7% (10 year term investment), and based on this scenario the death benefit reduced to $2.68M at policy year 10. However, the deposit did not happened as indicated in the attached quote from 2013. The total deposit amount is so far $849,674.91 made through 2013-2020, and the investment assumption had also changed since then. We ran a policy projection back in 2020 with an assumption of 2.75% and no further deposit into the policy, and the death benefit at policy year 10 was $2.29M. Given the lower total deposit amount and investment changes over time, this seems a reasonable projection of the policy. As I mentioned, the face amount will grind down to $10,000 over time (page 7 of 23), and your cash value will grow tax sheltered and make up the most of death benefit in the future. Please let us know if you would like to obtain the current dated projection for your records.

You are correct in pointing out that the face amount of your policy follows a declining schedule. The purpose of this design is to gradually reduce the face amount from its initial value of $4,986,000 to $10,000 over time, resulting in a decrease in the associated cost of insurance.

However, it's important to note that the death benefit consists of both the face amount and the cash value of the policy. The main objective of this policy type is to ultimately rely on your own premium payments and accumulated cash value to compose the majority of the death benefit. Initially, the face amount is higher while the cash value is lower. As time goes on, this allocation shifts towards a lower face amount and higher cash value.

The initial plan was to deposit $1,000,000 over the first 5 years ($200,000 per year for 5 years). We based this calculation on an investment assumption of 2.7% for a 10-year term investment. According to this scenario, the death benefit was projected to decrease to $2.68M by the 10th policy year. However, the actual deposit made did not match the plan outlined in the attached quote from 2013. As of 2013-2020, the total deposit amount stands at $849,674.91, and the investment assumption has changed since then.

We conducted a policy projection in 2020, assuming no further deposits into the policy and using an investment assumption of 2.75%. Based on this projection, the death benefit at the 10th policy year is estimated to be $2.29M. Taking into account the lower total deposit amount and the changes in investment assumptions over time, this projection appears to be a reasonable estimation of the policy's future performance. As mentioned earlier, the face amount will gradually decrease to $10,000 over time (as described on page 7 of 23), while your cash value will grow tax-sheltered and contribute significantly to the death benefit in the future.

If you would like to receive the most up-to-date projection for your records, please let us know.
hello, Can you please improve the following sentense?
Of course! Please provide the sentence that requires improvement.

IA does not care about the beneficial ownership nor the tax planning strategies that was implemented for the client, nor the fact the alter ego trust did not have the trust number. As you indicated in your email, the family trust is a new legal entity separate from the Alter Ego Trust. IA account was open legitimately with the Aldis Chinnery Alter Ego Trust deed. If we ask IA to change the name to the Darcy Chinnery Family Trust then this new legal entity is taking over the ownership of the account hence the disposition and tax slip. There’s no correction to be done here. IA is a financial institution following the rules and guidelines for each transaction they process. They are doing exactly what they suppose to do.

IA does not consider the beneficial ownership or the implemented tax planning strategies for the client, nor does it take into account that the alter ego trust lacks a trust number. As mentioned in your email, the family trust is a separate legal entity from the Alter Ego Trust. The IA account was legitimately opened under the Aldis Chinnery Alter Ego Trust deed. If we request IA to change the name to the Darcy Chinnery Family Trust, it signifies a transfer of ownership to this new legal entity, which would entail a disposition and tax slip. Therefore, there is no need for any correction in this matter. IA is a financial institution that adheres to the rules and guidelines governing each transaction they handle. They are fulfilling their obligations accordingly.
Thank you. can you please make the following sentence more professional?
Certainly! Please provide the sentence you would like to be improved for a more professional tone.

Thank you for arranging this, Genna. However if Nuvero wants to have a call for something they messed up, they can arrange a call by themselves. Why are we wasting our precious manpower to make it work for them?

Thank you, Genna, for coordinating this. However, if Nuvero wishes to address their mistakes through a call, it would be appropriate for them to arrange it on their own. It raises concerns as to why we are allocating our valuable resources to accommodate their needs.
can you make it little less professional?
Thanks for handling this, Genna. But if Nuvero wants to sort out something they messed up, they can arrange their own call. Why are we wasting our manpower to fix it for them?
sounds great. thank you
You're welcome! I'm glad I could help. If you have any more sentences that need improvement, feel free to ask.
how can make this sound less aggressive? "We are not here to accommodate their needs with our manpower"
Our manpower is not intended to be solely dedicated to accommodating their needs.
can you make it little less formal?
Our team's time and resources are better utilized elsewhere instead of solely catering to their needs.
still too much
Our team's time and resources are better spent on other priorities rather than solely catering to their needs.
make the original sentence little more friendly

We're here to help, but it might be more appropriate for Nuvero to arrange their own call if they need assistance with something they may have overlooked. Allocating our manpower for their needs might impact our other priorities.
can you please improve the following sentence
Of course! Please provide the sentence that needs improvement.

It would depend on what your plan is with this fund.  Would there be an immediate need for something or are you considering more of an long term investment that can be accessed at any time? Do you currently have the TFSA contribution room? If yes, I’d recommend to max out the TFSA contribution room first.  There are still funds left after the contribution to TFSA, then you could consider depositing it into the policy. Your policy currently has a deposit room of appox. $144,000. The policy is a great way to grow your investment tax-sheltered however the accessibility to the funds is somewhat limited – the most tax efficient way to access the fund would be to leveraging it like you already did. You could take advantage of interest deduction but you also need to consider the increase in interest payment (affordability). You could certainly withdraw the funds directly out of the policy then the amount withdrawn will be subject to taxation (income inclusion, not capital gain). +

It would be helpful to understand your plans for this fund. Are you looking for immediate access or considering a long-term investment that can be accessed at any time? Additionally, I would recommend checking if you currently have available TFSA contribution room. If so, I suggest maximizing your TFSA contribution before considering depositing into the policy.

After contributing to your TFSA, if there are still funds remaining, you could then consider depositing them into the policy. Your policy currently has a deposit room of approximately $144,000. While the policy offers tax-sheltered growth for your investment, it's important to note that accessing the funds is somewhat limited. Leveraging, as you have already done, can provide tax advantages, but it's crucial to consider the increase in interest payments and overall affordability.

If needed, you do have the option to withdraw funds directly from the policy. However, it's important to note that any amount withdrawn will be subject to income taxation rather than capital gains.
another sentence please:  if not,  after maximizing the TFSA, you could consider depositing into both RBC non-registered account and policy.

If you don't have available TFSA contribution room, once you've maximized your TFSA contribution, you can explore the option of depositing funds into both an RBC non-registered account and the policy.
can you please check the grammar for this sentence?
Sure! The grammar of the sentence "If not, after maximizing the TFSA, you could consider depositing into both RBC non-registered account and policy" is correct.

That sounds great. Our next step will be to meet virtually to complete the insurance application. During this meeting, we will go through the questionnaire together, and it is expected to take approximately one hour. I am available for the meeting on either Tuesday, September 12th, between 12 PM and 4 PM, or Friday, September 15th, between 10 AM and 1 PM. Please let me know if either of these dates and times work for you and Hunter to meet via TEAMS.
How about the above sentence?
